ety: fem. of darzī; ˚an or ˚in = S. inī
def: A sempstress; — a tailor's wife.
s.m. A sickle; a scythe (cf. dāsā).
s.m. (f. -ī), A fisherman; a boatman.
adj. Ten
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Ten years old; for ten years, decennial
obl. pl., or pl. formative, of das, The whole ten, all ten, the ten
s.f. = daśoṅ diśā; see s.v. daś for this and other compounds.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀲 (dasa), from Sanskrit दश (dáśa).

def: s.f.m.(?) The string by which scales are suspended or held in the hand; — the threads of the warp at the end of a piece of cloth which are left unwoven; the end of a piece of cloth.
s.m. corr. of dās, q.v.
s.f. corr. of dahshat, q.v.
s.m. A reaping-hook, a sickle.
s.m. A broad and long stone or piece of wood; — a piece of wood projecting from a wall to support the ćhappar or thatch; — a wall-plate.
s.m. = dāsā, and dās, qq.v.
s.f. = daśā, q.v.
s.f. State, condition, circumstances, predicament, plight; period or time of life (as youth, manhood, &c.), age
subs: s.m. Result of circumstances or of condition of life
s.m. Special or peculiar circumstances or condition
adj. In bad condition or circumstances, ill-conditioned, in wretched plight, unfortunate.

def: s.m. A slave, servant; a Śūdra; a suffix (orig. to the names of Śūdras, but now) to the names of all Hindūs except Brāhmans
subs: (˚sa+an˚), s.m. A slave of a slave, a servant of servants (applied in lowliness or humility by a speaker to himself)
s.f. The female slave of a slave; — s.m. Male and female slaves.

def: adj. Ten
subs: (˚śa+ar˚), s.m. Name of a warrior tribe descended from Daśārha of the family of Yadu; — the country inhabited by this tribe (in the south of India)
(˚śa+an˚), adj. Ten fingers long; — s.m. A water-melon, a melon
(˚śa+av˚), s.m. 'He of the ten incarnations,' an epithet of Vishnu (of whom the Hindūs believe there were ten descents from heaven for the protection or the punishment of mankind)
daś-pur, s.m. Name of a district or part of Mālwa in Bundelkhand; — a species of Cyperus rotundus
s.m. 'Having ten chariots'; name of an ancient sovereign of Ayodhyā (the modern Oude), and father of Rāma
daś-sat, adj. & s.m. Ten hundred; — a thousand
adj. & s.m. Ten thousand; — a myriad
vulg. daś-śīs, s.m. 'Ten-headed'; an epithet of Rāvan

s.m. Ten vices arising from kāma or love of pleasure, viz., hunting, gambling, sleeping by day, fault-finding (or abusiveness), lust, drunkenness, dancing, singing, playing, and useless travel (or, acc. to some, hypocrisy)
(˚tha+ari), or daś-kanṭha-jit, s.m. 'The enemy, or the conqueror, of Daśkanṭh,' an epithet of Rāma
s.m. A district, or a collection, of ten villages
(f. -ā), or daś-guṇā (f. -ī), adj. & adv. Ten-fold, ten times; ten times as large; ten times over
s.m. A collection of ten garlands
daś-mukh, daś-munḍā, s.m. 'Ten-faced'; an epithet of Rāvan
s.m. Name of a tonic medicine prepared from the roots of ten plants
vulg. dasoṅ, adj. The ten of them, all ten, the whole ten
daśoṅ-dig, s.f. The ten regions or quarters of the world
s.m. The ten gates or passages for the action of the faculties, viz. the eyes, the ears, the nostrils, the mouth, the crown of the head, the anus, and the penis
(˚śa+īśa), s.m. Lord of ten towns, superintendent of ten villages
or daśendriyā, s.m. pl. The ten organs of perception and action, viz. the skin, eye, tongue, nose, ear, larynx (or, acc. to some, the organ of speech), hand, foot, anus, and pudendum.

def: s.m. Expenses attending the Dasahrā festival; a cess levied by a zemīndar on his tenants (consisting of rice, milk, ghee, &c.).
s.m. See dasahrā.
(dialec.) = daswīṅ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Of or appertaining to servants; — a slave, servant ( = dās).
adj. Tenth ( = daswāṅ).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Tenth; — a tenth part.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Between ninety and one hundred years, very old or aged; — a very aged man.
s.f. The tenth day of a lunar fortnight; the tenth decade or last stage of a human life; the last ten years of a century.
s.f. The tenth day of the lunar fortnight ( = daswīṅ).
s.m. A tooth.
s.m. Bedding ( = ḍasaunā; bićhaunā).
s.m. A tooth.
v.t. To make a bed.
s.m. The plant Croton polyandrum.
s.m. = daśarath, q.v.s.v. daś.
s.m. See s.v. daśa, daś.
adj. & s.m. Belonging to Daśa-rath, coming from Daśarath; — a descendant of Daśarath; — a patronymic of Rāma.
s.m. See s.v. daśa, daś.
s.m. Name of a country in Central India (south-east of the Vindhaya hills).
s.f. Name of a river rising in the Vindhya hills (prob. the modern Dhasān; the ancient Dosarene).
s.f. A collection or aggregate of ten, a decade.
s.f. The condition of a slave or servant; slavery, servitude, service.
s.f. 1˚ = daśat, q.v.; — 2˚ A hundred.
= dasotarā, q.v.
s.m. = dāsatā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍasaunā, q.v.
s.m. Bedding, bed.
s.m. An offering made to a goddess when a child reaches its tenth year.
s.m. A panegyrist, a bard.
s.m. A panegyrist, a bard.
s.m. Name of the province of Mālwā.
s.m. = dāśeya, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), The son of a fisherman's wife.

ety: prob. fr. Zend rt. daś = S. daṅś; cf. S. दशन
def: s.m. A dagger, poniard.

ety: v.n. fr. dāshtan; rt. Zend dar = S. dhar
def: s.f. Bringing up, taking care of, keeping, breeding; care, patronage; — service
s.m. A desert, a steppe, an arid plain; a forest
subs: dasht-navard, adj. & s.m. Wandering or travelling over deserts; — a traverser of deserts
s.f. The wandering or travelling over deserts
s.m. A desert void of all but God, a terror-striking wilderness.

def: adj. Of the desert or forest; wild, savage.
s.f. A female slave or servant; a servant-maid
subs: dāsī-jāyā, s.m. The son of a female slave; a bastard.

def: s.f. A fisherwoman; the wife or daughter of a fisherman.
s.f. Thread; the unwoven threads at the end of a piece of cloth.
adj. & s.m. Having ten; — lord of ten towns, superintendent of ten villages ( = daśeś, q.v.s.v. daś).
s.f. = dasī, q.v.
s.f. corr. of dāsī, q.v.
s.f. = dāsī, q.v.
adj. = daśīlā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), In good circumstances, prosperous, fortunate.
= P دسخطي dasḵẖat̤ī, contrac. of dast-ḵẖat̤, &c., qq.v.s.v. dast.
= P دسخط dasḵẖat̤, contrac. of dast-ḵẖat̤, &c., qq.v.s.v. dast.

ety: ḍās˚ = S. ध्वंसय(ति), caus. of rt. ध्वंस्
def: v.t. To make a bed.
v.t. = ḍāṅsnā, q.v.

ety: ḍas˚ = Prk. डस(इ) or डंस(इ) = S. दंश(ति), rt. दंश् or दस(ति), rt. दस्
def: v.t. To bite, to sting (as a venomous animal).
s.m. Moulting (of birds)
adj. See s.v. das, and daś.
adj. See s.v. daś, and das.
s.m. = dasokhā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Ten per cent.; — a tenth.
s.m. See s.v. daś.
s.m. The fragrant grass Cyperus rotundus.
s.m. = dāśpur, q.v.
s.m. = dāśpur, q.v.
s.f. (local), 'Hand and hip'; name of a twist or manœuvre in wrestling.
s.m. An inferior class of the Vaiśya caste; — a hybrid, half-caste.

ety: old P. daśta; Zend zaśta; S. hasta
def: s.m. Hand (syn. hāth); a cubit; — suit (of armour or clothes); anything complete (as a house with its offices); purging or loose stool, stool, purge, evacuation
subs: adj. & s.m. Extending or moving the hands in dancing, or in relinquishing anything, or in anger or excitement, or in denying; — one who moves the hands in dancing, &c.
adj. 'Accustomed to the hand,' tame, familiarized, gentle, tractable; — handy; skilful, dexterous
v.n. To have diarrhœa or loose bowels
adj. & s.m. Intermeddling, interfering; — one who interferes, &c.; one who commences a business; — an encroacher; an oppressor, a plunderer
s.f. Putting the hand (to, -meṅ, anything), meddling (with), interference; encroachment; oppression
(-meṅ), To put the hand (to), to meddle, &c.

s.f. 'That which ties or fastens the hands'; what a man takes with him as a means of promoting his suit; what one gets into his hand and depends on; a signature; a note of hand, bond, deed, title-deed, voucher, certificate, instrument, charter, &c.; — a small present to be given into the hands of a person whose favour is sought; — instance or example at hand
May good attend the hand (said by one who points out on his own body the seat of another's malady or pain)
adj. & adv. Hand in hand; from hand to hand; quick, expeditious; — s.m. ( = dast ba-dast muʻāmala), Ready-money purchase or transaction
To put up the hands in prayer
s.m. Pre-excellence, superiority, advantage, victory (at play, in war or law); encroachment, usurpation; plunder, rapine; misappropriation, embezzlement
(-se), To give up, abandon, relinquish, resign, to wash (one's) hands (of), to leave off, to let go, to desert; to abstain, desist (from), cease; to retire; to decline
s.f. Relinquishment, abandonment, renunciation; withdrawal (from); cessation
To be quite helpless, be unable to execute (one's) intention; to be in great grief, be distressed, weak, poor, or wretched
adv. With close or folded hands (in token of respect)
To salute (in the Eastern manner) by putting the hand to the head
adj. & adv. 'Hand to the hilt,' sword in hand, ready for battle
s.m. Cloth in which a small bundle is wrapped up
s.m. A string of pearls or precious stones worn (by women) on the wrist; — a gold or silver ornament worn on the back of the hand
To kiss the hand, to salute
s.f. Lightness of finger; stealth; — a civil war; a duel:-dast-ḵẖat̤, vulg. daskhat, daskat, s.m. 'Hand-writing'; signature; endorsement, or acceptance; — a manuscript, chirography
adj. Written with one's own hand; in manuscript; signed
adj. & s.m. 'Long-armed'; oppressive, extortionate; — an oppressor
s.f. Violence, oppression, extortion, exaction, excess
(-se, or -par?), To practice violence or oppression, to oppress, ill-treat, plunder, &c.

adj. Within (one's) reach or power; to be reached with the hand; able; come to hand, obtained; — s.f. Reach; ability, power, means; aid, assistance; facility; dexterity, skill; acquisition, attainment
v.n. To come to hand, to arrive; to be reached; to be obtained; to have in one's power, to be able
'Having healing in his hand,' an epithet of a physician who is successful in restoring his patients to health
s.m. lit. 'The hidden hand'; a charm or incantation by means of which a hidden hand supplies one's wants
s.m. A retailer, a hawker, a pedlar
s.f. Pedlary; the business of a pedlar
adj. Amputated in the hand; — s.m. A writer
adj. Wrought, manufactured; dexterous, expert, expeditious; — s.m. One who lives by the work of his hands, an artificer, handicraftsman, artizan, artist, a manufacturer
s.f. Manufacture, handicraft; art, trade; operation (in surgery); dexterity
adj. & s.m. Taken by the hand; led; — oppressed, weak; — a led horse; a guide (of the blind), a leader; a beggar; a captive
or dast-gah, s.f. Power, strength, ability, means; understanding; intellect; knowledge
adj. & s.m.? Going from hand to hand; hawked about; — money, &c. which is obtained on loan, a loan for a short period (on a verbal promise to pay); — anything hawked about for sale
(-ko), To lend (money, &c.) for a short period
adj. & s.m. Taken by the hand, helped; — one who is taken by the hand, a protégé
adj. & s.m. Taking by the hand; laying hands on; imprisoned; — a helper, assistant, patron, protector, a guardian-saint; — a prisoner
s.f. Assistance, aid, help; defence, support, protection, patronage
s.m.? The first money received for sale of merchandize in the morning, handsel (syn. bonī)
(ko), To feel a desire to ease nature; to have loose stools
s.m. lit. 'Hand-wiper'; a towel; napkin; handkerchief
dast-nigarān, adj. lit. 'Regarding the hand'; needy, wanting, in want
'To have the hand on the collar,' to engage in combat
adj. Attained, obtained; reached; attainable, procurable; — dast-yāb honā, v.n. To be attained, be acquired, be found, &c.; to attain (to), reach
s.m. A bracelet (of gold or silver).

def: s.m. Zinc, tutanag; lapis calaminaris.

s.m. A handle, helve, haft, hilt; a pestle; a handful, bundle; a bundle of twenty-four arrows; a quire (of paper); a hank or skein (of silk, thread, &c.), a clew; a nosegay; a flower-bed; a kind of button (sewn on the qabā or coat); division of an army, brigade, squadron
subs: s.m. Commander of a division or brigade, a brigadier.

def: s.f. The strap which depends from the hilt of a sword; — a falconer's glove ( = dastakī).

s.f. 'A little hand'; a handle; a knock or rap (at a door, &c.); clapping of the hands (com. with a view of drawing a person's attention); — a pass, passport; permit, license; a summons, citation, warrant, judicial writ, process; fee for serving a summons, &c.; commission; tax
subs: (-par), To knock (at a door, &c.); to clap the hands
(-par), To fix or attach a summons, &c. (to a door, wall, &c.); to impose or levy a tax (on).

def: s.m. Fee for a passport, or for serving a writ or summons.
s.f. A falconer's glove; — a pocket-book; a tweezer-case.
s.f. A story, fable, tale; history; — fame, notoriety
subs: A story-teller, a reciter of tales.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَاسْتَان (dāstān). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi داستاں (dastaṉ).

def: s.f. = dāstān, q.v.

ety: dast, q.v.+suff. -āna = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: s.m. A glove, a gauntlet; a falconer's glove; the handle of a shield.
s.f. A sash or fine muslin cloth wrapped round a turban; — a napkin, towel; a cloth, &c. (on which dishes are laid)
subs: s.m. A servant whose business it is to make up the turban
s.m. A cloth, &c. to place dishes on (vulg. dastar-ḵẖẉān, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَسْتَار (dastār), from دَسْت (dast) + ـَار (-ār). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi دستار (dstar /⁠dastār⁠/).

def: s.m. A small turban or sash; — a napkin; towel; handkerchief.
s.m. A table-cloth, a piece of cloth or leather, &c. spread on the ground, upon which the dishes of a meal are placed
subs: 'The cat of the table-cloth'; an uninvited guest, a sponge.

def: s.f. See s.v. dast.
s.f. See s.v. dast.
s.m. Contrac. of dasūṭhan, q.v.

adj. Of or from the hand, manual; — having a handle, handled; — s.f. A small handle; a vessel with a handle or handles; any vessel, &c., that may be lifted by or carried in the hand; a small pen-case; a torch or link carried in the hand; a trick with the hand (in wrestling); a present to native officials at the dasahrā festival.

ety: dast, q.v.+ūr = var = Zend bar = S. भर्
def: s.m. A prime-minister ( = vazīr); a councillor, a senator a model, exemplar, rule, regulation; basis, foundation settled or established order, custom, usage, practice, fashion, mode, manner; a customary fee or tax, or percentage; — a district, an aggregate of parganas
subs: s.m. Model, rule, regulation; rules of practice, body of instructions, manual of regulations, hand-book, manual; code of laws; constitution, form of government
adv. According to custom, &c. ( = next, q.v.)
adv. According to rule or custom, as usual, as heretofore, in statu quo
adj. Without example or precedent, unprecedented, irregular, informal, anomalous, unlawful.

def: s.m. pl. (of dastūr, after the model of the Ar.), Customs, usages, practices, modes; customary perquisites, fees, per-centage.

adj. Customary, usual, ordinary; — s.f. Perquisites paid to servants by one who sells to their master (usually at the rate of one or two pice in the rupee), perquisites, fees, commission of a broker; — leave, license, congé
subs: adv. In the customary way, in ordirary.

def: s.m. One who takes dastūrī; a broker.
s.m. See s.v. dast.
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Bathing on the tenth day after child-birth.

ety: Prk. दसमओ, or दसवँओ; S. दश+तम+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -wīṅ), Tenth; — a tenth part, a tenth; — the ten days during which alms, &c. are given for the benefit of the soul of a deceased relative; prayers or offerings on the tenth day (after a death, &c.).
adj. = daswāṅ, q.v.
s.m. A tenth part (added to the revenue, &c.).
s.f. Tenth day of a month; — tenth day of the lunar fortnight ( = dasamī).
s.m. Slavery, servitude, service.
s.m. A cruel or mischievous man; an enemy; an oppressor, wrong-doer; a thief, robber, bandit, ruffian; an outcast; name of one of the mixed classes
subs: s.f. Dishonest mode of living, theft, &c.

def: s.m. Bountifulness, liberality.
s.m. corr. of dāṅt, q.v.

s.f. Restraining, restraint, checking, check, snubbing, brow-beating, threatening, threat, menacing gesture; — a stopper, stopple, cork, plug, spigot, spike; key-stone (of an arch; cf. ḍāṅṭ or ḍāṅth, ḍāṅṭhal, ḍāṅṭhī, ḍaṭṭā, ḍanthal)
subs: s.f. Threat, menacing gesture; attack, assault, charge, rush or gallop (upon or against; cf. ḍhāṭī)
(-meṅ), To put a stopple or plug, &c. (in), to plug, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Giving, bestowing, imparting; liberal, generous; — giver, donor, benefactor, a liberal man
s.f. = dāṅtan, datwan, qq.v.
v.t. To restrain, curb, rein in, stop, check, make stationary, &c. (see ḍaṭnā, and ḍāṭnā).
v.t. = dabkānā, q.v.

ety: pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Liberality, beneficence; benevolence.
s.m. = dātā.
contrac. of dātār, q.v. (used in comp.).
s.m. A large rake (used for gathering high grass together into a cocklet).
adj. & s.m. To be given or bestowed; — anything that may be, or ought to be, given.
s.f. Liberality, generosity
s.m. A stalk, &c. ( = ḍāṅṭh, ḍanṭhā, &c.).

ety: fr. dat = S. दन्त; or akin to ḍāṭnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To face, confront; to oppose, to fight, to attack; to confute.
v.t. = datnā, q.v.; — v.n. To stop; to be confronted.

ety: ḍāṭ˚ = Prk. उट्ठ(इ), fr.डट्ठ = S. दष्ट
def: v.t. To restrain, curb, check, rein in (a horse, &c.); to chide, rebuke, threaten, snub, brow-beat; to correct, chastise, punish; — to stop up, to plug; to fill (as a ćilam, &c.), to stuff, cram ( = ṭhūṅsnā).

v.n. To be restrained, be checked; to stop, stand still, be or become stationary; to halt, stay, remain: to be or become fixed or firm; to last, endure; to be settled or agreed on; to be determined or resolved.
s.f. Land which has been lately cropped with the Indian corn, or bājrā, or joʼār.
s.m. = dātā-pan, q.v.
s.m. An instrument for cutting or chopping; a sort of sickle; a large knife, a bill-hook, or hatchet (see dā).
adj. & s.m. = dātā, q.v.
s.f. = S داترتو दातृत्व dātr̤itva, s.m. Beneficence, bountifulness, liberality.
s.m. = S داترتا दातृता dātr̤itā, s.f. Beneficence, bountifulness, liberality.
s.f. A girl who is given in marriage.
s.m. = ḍaṭṭā, q.v.
s.m. A stopper, stopple, cork, plug, spigot ( = ḍāṭ); a fence.
part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Given, granted, presented, made over, assigned; — a common name or title of a man of the Vaiśya caste, and also of some Kāyasths
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Self-given; — one who has given himself; a youth or orphan who gives himself to persons disposed to adopt him

def: s.m. A son given away by his natural parents to persons engaging to adopt him (one of the twelve kinds of heirs acknowledged by the old Hindū law).
s.f. Gift, donation, offering.
s.f. A match.
s.f. = 1˚ datvan, q.v.; — 2˚ Cleaning the teeth with a toothbrush.
s.m. A tooth-brush (com. a piece of stick or a twig of the nīm or babūl tree).
s.m. An appellation of a father or of an elder brother; — Baladeva, Kr̤ishṇa's elder brother.
s.f. Name of a kind of white wheat; name of a sort of garland.

adj. Relating to David, of David; — s.f. Chain armour (so called because it is believed to have been originally made by David), coat of mail; — name of a shrub that bears a flower like camomile, Chrysanthemum indicum; a firework in imitation of this shrub; — a kind of white wheat ( = dāʼūd-ḵẖānī).
s.m. Daughter's son, grandson.
s.f. Daughter's daughter, granddaughter.

r: daʻuī, daʻwạ̄, daʻwā ḵẖārij karnā, kā, daʻwe-dār, daʻwī-dār
def: s.m. A pretender; a claimant, suitor, plaintiff
subs: (with gen. of thing, and loc., -par, of person on whom claim is made), To lay claim (to), to claim, make a claim (on, -par, for, -kā), to sue; to make pretensions (to), to pretend, assert, profess.

def: s.m. Shape, form, fashion, figure, make, build; appearance, aspect; natural form or state, nature, constitution; course, way, method, manner, mode, style, air, general features or character (of speech, &c.); pattern, model, specimen; opportunity; device, scheme, plan; estimate of revenue or assets (as a guide to assessment); raised boundary (of a field, &c.), boundary, limit ( = ḍaulā; cf. ḍhār); — coition, copulation ( = ḍaulā)
subs: s.m. A model-form, a blank form or return (to be used as a model)
(-ko), To bring into shape, give shape or form to, put in order, regulate, systematize
(-kā), To set the example (of), lay the foundation (of)
adv. In due form, in order, ship-shape, methodically, &c.
adj. Without shape or form, shapeless, irregular, ill-shaped, ill-formed, ill-made, ugly, deformed; uncouth, ill-mannered, cross-grained.

def: s.m. Manner, &c.; boundary; coition (prob. fr. ḍol-nā).

ety: for A. دولة, inf. n. of un. of دول 'to come round,' &c.
def: s.f. Good fortune, prosperity, happy state or condition, happiness, felicity; riches, wealth; — state, government, monarchy, empire, sovereignty, dominion, rule; — cause, occasion, effect, means
subs: s.m. lit. 'House of fortune'; a mansion, palace, house (in polite speech, e.g. āp-kā daulat-ḵẖāna kahāṅ hai, 'Where is your house?')
A royal residence, the king's palace
adj. & s.m. Wishing happiness or prosperity or empire, loyal; — a well-wisher, a loyal subject (com. applied by the writer of a letter or petition to himself in the sense of 'I' or 'me')
s.f. The wishing happiness or prosperity or empire, good wishes, goodwill, loyalty
s.m. Seat of good fortune, abode of felicity; seat of sovereignty
adj. & s.m. Fortunate, prosperous, happy; wealthy, opulent, rich; — a rich person, &c.
v.t. To bless or prosper (with, -se), to enrich (with)
s.f. Prosperity, happiness; wealthiness, wealth, opulence
s.m. Possessor of wealth, a rich man
(-kī), postpn. By the good fortune (of), by means (of), by the favour (of), through
s.m. A novus homo, a parvenu, an upstart.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَولَت (dawlat), borrowed from Arabic دَوْلَة (dawla).

def: adj. & s.m. = daulat-mand, q.v.s.v. daulat.
s.f. A forest-conflagration; — fire, flame; burning heat, excess of heat or thirst
subs: (-kī), To feel burning heat; to be consumed with the flame of love, to long or pine (for).

ety: S. दा 'protection'+मञ्चं, and मञ्चकं; — cf. ḍāmćā
def: s.m. An elevated platform or shed raised on bamboos in a field or orchard (where a watchman is stationed to protect the crop from cattle, birds, &c.); a scaffold, platform.
s.m. A scaffold, &c. ( = ḍaunjā, q.v.).

ety: S. दा 'protection'+मञ्चं, and मञ्चकं; — cf. ḍāmćā
def: s.m. An elevated platform or shed raised on bamboos in a field or orchard (where a watchman is stationed to protect the crop from cattle, birds, &c.); a scaffold, platform.

ety: inf. n. of دور 'to turn round, to revolve,' & c.
def: s.m. Going round, moving in a circle, revolving; revointion (of a body, or of time); circular motion; the going round, or circulating (of wine); the cup handed round; the coming round in turn (of days or times); vicissitude; — repetition (of a lesson); a kind of argument, reasoning in a circle; — circumference, perimeter; circular enclosure; border (of a garment, &c.); circle, circuit; orbit; circuit of rule, compass, jurisdiction, power, authority, dominion, sway; — a period of years, time, age, cycle; a turn, tour, round, course, progress; a turn or twist (of a rope, &c.)
subs: s.m. Circuit of rule, jurisdiction, dominion, sway ( = daur)
To go or come round; to complete a period (of time, &c.); to make the circuit (of, -kā); to encircle, encompass, surround; to go through, to repeat (a lesson, or the Qorʼān, &c.).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀽𑀭 (dūra), from Sanskrit दूर (dūrá). Also influenced by Classical Persian دور (dūr).

def: s.m. The strings used to sling a basket for irrigation.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀽𑀭 (dūra), from Sanskrit दूर (dūrá). Also influenced by Classical Persian دور (dūr).

def: s.f. Running, a run; gallop; pace; race (chiefly in comp., e.g. ghuṛ-daur); course, career; range, reach, compass; endeavour, effort, struggle; an expedition, incursion, invasion, attack, assault, raid; — (past. conj. part. of dauṛnā), adv. Running, in haste, quickly, readily, eagerly ( = dauṛ-ke)
subs: s.f. Running, haste, hurry, bustle; contest in running, race
v.n. To come running, to come quickly, &c.
s.f. A running to and fro, bustling about, exertion, labour and fatigue, much effort
To run to and fro, to use much effort or exertion, to toil hard
To make a forced march; to make a sudden attack (on, -par); to surprise.

def: s.m. A large basket (without a lid); cf. daurī.
s.m. A runner, &c. ( = dauṛāhā, q.v.); — a mounted highwayman.
s.m. = daurā, q.v.

ety: for A. دورة, fr. daur, q.v.
def: s.m. A going round, a revolution, a turn; round, tour, circuit, course, &c. (see daur)
subs: Commitment for trial before the circuit sessions
daure-ko uṭhnā, v.n. To go on a tour or circuit; to hold sessions.

def: s.m. = dauṛāhā, q.v.
s.m. A runner, a village messenger, a courier, a harbinger, a guide.
s.m. A forest conflagration (cf. davār).

ety: dauṛ(nā)+āk = Prk. आवको or आअको = S. आपकः (i.e. the caus. augment āpi+कः)
def: s.m. A fast runner; a fleet horse, a racer; a courier.

v.t. To cause to run, to make (a horse) gallop, to gallop; to run, drive, impel, actuate, expedite, speed, despatch, send (after, or in pursuit); to run (in, -men), put (in) quickly (as bread into an oven, &c.); to set (thought) busy, to give wings to thought, or fancy, e.g. ḵẖayāl dauṛānā
subs: v.t. To cause to run in haste; to despatch with all speed.

def: s.m. = dauṛāhā, q.v.
s.m. Evil-mindedness, badness of soul, wickedness, depravity.
s.m. Impotence, feebleness, weakness, debility.
s.m. Bad condition, miserable plight, ill-luck, misfortune.
s.f. Bad or disagreeable smell, badness of smell, fetor.

ety: for दिवडओ, by transposn. fr. Prk. अड्ढदुइओ = S. अर्द्ध+द्वितीय+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Half as much again ( = ḍeʼoṛhā); — raised one half higher (a tone in music).
s.f. = ḍeʼoṛhī, q.v.
s.f. A drawn game at ćaupaṛ.

s.f. A rope with which a string of cattle are bound together (as bullocks, when threshing, &c.); a ashing-line; a line with which lands are measured ( = ḍoṛī); — a small flat basket (without a lid); a sling-basket (used in irrigation).
s.m. Wickedness, maliciousness, vileness, depravity.
adv. Running, in haste, quickly, readily, eagerly.
adv. Running, in haste, quickly, readily, eagerly.

ety: dauṛ˚ = Prk. दवड(इ) or दवडे(इ), fr. दवड = S. द्रव+र
def: v.n. To run, to go quickly, to hasten; to gallop, course; to drive; to rush (at, -par), fall (upon), assault, attack, invade; to spread quickly, travel fast (as news); to spread (through, -meṅ), to pervade (as poison, in the system)
subs: v.n. To run violently, to run to and fro, &c. ( = dauṛ-dhup karnā, q.v.s.v. dauṛ.

def: s.m. = ḍamrū, q.v.
s.m. Day; dawn, early morning.
s.m. Day, dawn ( = daus, q.v.).
s.m. = dāṅw, q.v.

ety: fr. dav-ī-dan, 'to run'; rt. S. धाव्
def: part. adj. Running (used in comp.)
subs: dav-ā-davish, dav-ā-davī or dauʼī, s.f. Running incessantly; running to and fro, or in every direction; bustling about; being active and energetic in any business; bustle; labour and fatigue, toil and trouble (syn. tag-ā-pū; dauṛ-dhūp).
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi दो (do), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑁄 (do), from Sanskrit द्व (dvá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dwáH, from Proto-Indo-European *dwóh₁.

def: s.m. = دعوي daʻwạ̄, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. دوي 'to be diseased'; or fr. داوي, 'to treat medically,' conj. iii of دوي
def: s.f. Medicine; a remedy
subs: adj. Medicable, admitting of a remedy, remediable, curable
s.m. A room for medicines, a medical hall, an apothecary's shop; a dispensary
s.f., or dawā-darman, s.f. Use of medicine, application of a remedy; medical treatment; medicine, remedy
(-kī), To treat medically, to apply or use a remedy
s.m. A maker or compounder of medicines, an apothecary
s.m. A seller of medicines, a druggist
(-kī), To treat medically, to apply a remedy, to remedy, cure, heal

r: daʻwạ̄, daʻuī, daʻwā ḵẖārij karnā, kā, daʻwe-dār, daʻwī-dār
def: s.m. A pretender; a claimant, suitor, plaintiff
subs: (with gen. of thing, and loc., -par, of person on whom claim is made), To lay claim (to), to claim, make a claim (on, -par, for, -kā), to sue; to make pretensions (to), to pretend, assert, profess.

def: s.m. A forest conflagration, a forest on fire; — a forest; — fire, heat (see dāh)
subs: (˚va+ag˚) or dāvānal (˚va+an˚), s.m. A forest conflagration.

def: s.m. Fire; burning; a wood on fire, a forest conflagration, &c. (see dāv)
subs: (˚va+ag˚), and davānal (˚va+an˚), s.m. A forest conflagration
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi दो (do), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑁄 (do), from Sanskrit द्व (dvá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dwáH, from Proto-Indo-European *dwóh₁.

def: adj. (dialec.) Left (hand or side).
s.m. corr. of dāyā (fr. dāʼī, 'nurse'), q.v.
s.m. A large (wooden) spoon, a ladle.
s.m. pl. (of dābba), Beasts, cattle; (as a sing.) an animal, a beast, a quadruped.
s.f. See s.v. dav, or dau, 'running.'
s.f. See s.v. dav, or dau, 'running.'
adj. = ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. dava, dav.
s.f. = dawā, q.v.

r: dawāʼir, dāʼira, dawāʼir mutawāzī ḵẖat̤t̤-ě-istiwā, dawāʼir-ě-ham-markaz
def: s.m. pl. (of dāʼira), Circles

r: davāl, duvāl, duʼāl, doʼāl, divāl, dabba, ḍab, ḍāb
def: s.f. Skin, hide, leather; a leather strap, stirrup-leather, a belt; — splendour, pomp, grandeur, majesty
subs: adj. & s.m. Web-footed; leather-legged; — name of a people in India who have thin and ductile legs with which they strangle unwary travellers (they are said to pretend to be lame, and to importune travellers to carry them on their backs, which proves fatal to those whose compassion induces them to comply, as the villains twist their legs round their necks and instantly strangle them), the Thags; — a member of that class, a Thag, q.v.

def: s.m. pl. (of dūlāb = P. dol-āb), Wheels, water-wheels.
s.m. Continuing, lasting; persevering; continuance; perpetuity; duration; — adv. Continually, always, perpetually, eternally
s.m. corr. of dāman, q.v.
s.m. The act or process of treading out corn (cf. dāʼeṅ); — destruction.
adj. (dialec.) Left (hand or side).

ety: act. part. of davīdan, and = S. धावन; cf. dav
def: part. adj. Running, walking fast.

ety: for davanda, act. part. of davīdan, and = S. धाव+अन्त्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Running; — a fast runner; a fleet horse, a racer.
adj. = ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, q.v.
s.f. corr. of dāmanī, q.v.
s.m. (f. ?), lit. 'Forest-enemy'; a fire that breaks out in a forest, a forest conflagration ( = davānal, q.v.s.v. dava, dav).
wety: From Sanskrit द्वार (dvāra), from Proto-Indo-Iranian, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰworom (“enclosure, courtyard, i.e. something enclosed by the door”), from *dʰwor-, compare *dʰwer-.

def: s.m. = ḍābar, q.v.
s.m. God; — a just prince; a sovereign; a judge, administrator, arbiter.
s.f. Sovereignty; empire, dominion.

ety: for A. دعوة, inf. n. of un. fr. دعو 'to pray, to call,' &c.
def: s.f. A prayer, benediction; a call, invitation, convocation, call or invitation to Mohammadanism; invitation to a repast or feast; fare, repast, feast, banquet; invocation (of spirits), exorcism; — pretension, claim
subs: s.f. 'Feast of Samarcand'; a feast one day and a fast the next
s.f. 'Fare of Shīrāz'; simple fare, a plain repast
(-kī), To accept an invitation to a feast
(-kī), To give a feast; to mvite
v.n. To go to a feast
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَعْوَت (da'wat), borrowed from Arabic دَعْوَة (daʕwa), from دَعَا (daʕā).

ety: for A. دواة, fr. دوي; see dawā
def: s.f. An ink-holder, inkstand; an inkhorn, a portable case with receptacles for ink and writing-reeds, &c.; — ink
subs: s.f. The style with which the cloth or wool in an (Eastern) ink-bottle is stirred.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دوات (dawāt), from Arabic دَوَاة (dawāh). Compare Gujarati દવાત (davāt), Marathi दौत (daut), Punjabi ਦਵਾਤ (davāt) / دوات (doāt), Assamese দোৱাত (düat).

def: s.f. pl. (of daʻwat), Prayers, benedictions; convocations, invitations, &c.
s.m. One invited to a feast, a guest; — an invoker of spirits, exorcist, magician.
v.t. To tread out corn, to thresh, to tread; — s.m. The act of treading corn.

ety: for A. dawarān, inf. n. of دور 'to go or move round'
def: s.m. Going round, revolving, rolling round, whirling, &c.; circulation (as of the blood, &c.); — a revolution; a period, circle, cycle; time, an age; vicissitude; fortune
subs: s.m. A swimming in the head, dizziness, giddiness, vertigo.

def: s.f. (prov.); 1˚ = ḍibbī; 2˚ = ḍeʼoṛhī, qq.v.

ety: inf. n. of دوس 'to trample upon,' &c.
def: s.m. Trampling, treading on; beating (the ground with the feet); treading out (corn); beating, thrashing, drubbing; indulging much in venery; — polishing (a sword); — baseness, meanness.
adj. Turning round, revolving, rotating, moving in a circle, circling; — versatile, fickle (as fortune)
wety: From Sanskrit द्वार (dvāra), from Proto-Indo-Iranian, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰworom (“enclosure, courtyard, i.e. something enclosed by the door”), from *dʰwor-, compare *dʰwer-.

def: (dialec.), 1˚ = diyā, p. p. of denā; — 2˚ = deke, past. conj. part. of denā.
s.m. = dāya, dāy, q.v.

ety: Prk. दत्तिओ; S. दात्रि+कः; see dāʼī
def: s.m. The husband of a dāʼī or nurse; a male nurse.
s.f. A nurse, &c. ( = dāʼī, q.v.).
s.f. A gift ( = daʼī).
s.f. Compassion, pity, sympathy, tenderness, mercy, clemency, affection, love, favour, kindness, benevolence
subs: s.m. A pitiful or sympathetic person; a proper name
dayā-rūpī, adj. Of the nature, appearance, or form of compassion; compassionate, pitiful, merciful, tender, affectionate, kind, beneficent, generous, &c.
dayā-sindhu, s.m. 'Ocean of compassion, &c.' ( = dayā-nidhi, q.v.)
adj. (f. -ā), Connected with, or possessed of or endowed with, compassion, or mercy; compassionate, kind, &c.
or dayā-śīl, adj. (f. -ā), Of a compassionate disposition, compassionate, tender-hearted, merciful, affectionate, kind, &c.
(f. -ī), or dayā-kar (f. -ī), dayā-kr̤it (f. -ā), adj. Showing pity or compassion, sympathetic, pitiful, merciful, affectionate, kind, benevolent, beneficent, generous, liberal
adj. (f. matī), Compassionate, merciful, kind, &c. (see dayā-kar)
dayā-may, adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of, or abounding in, compassion, &c.
s.m. 'Treasure of mercy'; a very compassionate person; an epithet of the Deity
dayā-vat, dayā-van, or dayā-vant, adj. (f. vatī) = dayā-mān, q.v.
s.m. 'A hero of compassion,' a very compassionate or merciful person

def: s.m. Demand, claim; plaint.
s.m. A gift, present, donation; a nuptial present; — separate property (of a wife); paternal property, patrimony, inheritance
subs: dāyādav (˚ya+āda), s.m. An heir; a claimant; a son, or other kinsman, who is entitled to partake of an inheritance
dāya-vibhāg, s.m. Partition or portioning of mheritance, division of property amongst heirs; portion.

def: (old H.), = H دايا दाया dāyā, s.m. corr. of dayā, q.v.
= H داي दाय dāya (old H.), s.m. corr. of dayā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī) = S داياپورتن दायापवर्तन dāyāpavartan, s.m. See s.v. dāy.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Giving; — giver, donor (com. used as last member of Hindī comp.; e.g. bhikshā-dāyak, 'alms-giver'; — cf. dāʼī).
adj. Compassionate, pitiful, merciful, gracious, kind, beneficent ( = dayā-mān; dayā-śīl).
adj. See dayāl.
s.f. = S ديالتو दयालुत्व dayālutva, s.m. Compassionateness, pitifulness, tenderness, mercifulness, charity, kindness, beneficence, generousness.
s.m. = S ديالتا दयालुता dayālutā, s.f. Compassionateness, pitifulness, tenderness, mercifulness, charity, kindness, beneficence, generousness.
adj. & adv. Right (hand or side = dāhinā, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Crying on the right (a partridge, — considered as a bad omen by travellers and thieves).

def: s.m. Flying, flight (of a bird); — a car or litter carried upon men's shoulders, a palanquin, a 'doolie.'

r: ḍāyan, ḍāyin, ḍāʼiṅ, ḍāʼeṅ, ḍākin, ḍākinī
def: s.f. A female goblin, a witch, an old hag who is reputed to have an evil eye; an ugly old woman.
s.m. corr. of dīwān, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī) See s.v. dāy.

r: dayau, dayo, diyā, denā, daiyā, diyau, diyo
def: (Braj); 1˚ = diyā, p.p. of denā; — 2˚ = daiyā; — दियौ diyau, दियो diyo (Braj) = diyā (fr. denā).
adj. See s.v. dayā.
adj. See s.v. dayā.
adj. (f. -inī), Giving, presenting, granting; communicating; causing, effecting (com. used as last member of Hindī compounds; see dāʼī).
(dialect.) = dī, for diʼī, fem. of diyā, p.p. of denā.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Desired, cherished, beloved, dear; — a loved one; a husband, a lover.

ety: supp. to be a wrong reading for dāpita
def: part. Condemned to pay, fined; sentenced; adjudged, awarded.
s.f. A wife, spouse; mistress, sweetheart
subs: (˚tā+ādh˚), adj. & s.m. In subjection to a wife, hen-pecked; — a hen-pecked husband.

ety: for dewāṛā = S. देव+आल+कः; cf. dīmak
def: s.m. A hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, white-ant hill.

r: debā dībā, debāh, dev, bāft, lit
def: (contrac. of debāh = dev+bāft, qq.v.), s.m. lit. 'The weaving of the Devs or Jinn; a sort of silken texture of high price, silk brocade; gold tissue.
s.m. = debā, q.v.
s.m. = debāh or debā, q.v.
s.m. = debāh or debā, q.v.
s.m. The mast-tree, Uvaria longifolia.
s.f. A goddess, &c. ( = devī, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Left (hand, or side = dewrā; syn. bāyāṅ).
v.t. See s.v. denā.
adj. Shining intensely, glowing, very radiant or lustrous, blazing.
s.f. = deg, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. Zend diz = S. दिह् (देग्धि), cf. P. dez, 'a cooking pot'
def: s.f. A large metal pot (for culinary or other purposes), a caldron, kettle; a boiler
subs: s.m. A trivet, tripod, pot-hook; a fire-place
v.n. Pots and pans to resound, preparations for a feast to be going on.

def: s.m. Step, pace, stride ( = ḍig or ḍag, q.v.).

ety: deg, q.v.+dim. aff. ća = ak = S. क
def: s.m. A metal cooking utensil (smaller than a deg̠), a small caldron, a kettle.

ety: deg, q.v.+dim. aff. ća = ak = S. क
def: s.m. A metal cooking utensil (smaller than a deg̠), a small caldron, a kettle.
s.f. = degćā, q.v.
s.f. (but, about Benares, m., as in Prk. & S.), The body, the person
subs: (˚ha+āt˚), s.m. One who asserts that the body and soul are one, a materialist
(˚ha+an˚), s.m. The end of the body, i.e. death
(˚ha+an˚), s.m. Another body, another embodied state (in the doctrine of metempsychosis)
s.m. 'Resigning or relinquishing the body,' dying; death, voluntary death
part. adj. Separated or detached from the body (as excrement, or the spirit)
(-kī, or -apnī), To cover the person, conceal (one's) wickedness
adj. Occupying or assuming a body, embodied, incarnate
(apnī), To keep up the spirits; to be firm, to steady oneself, recover oneself
s.m. Decay or wasting of the body, sickness, disease
deh-vat, deh-vant, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Furnished with a body, embodied, corporeal; of or belonging to the body; — an embodied creature, a living being, a man ( = dehī)
s.f. 'Departure of the body,' dying, death.

def: s.m. A poetical form of deh, q.v.
adj. (f. -ikā), Of or belonging to the body.
s.f. Threshold of a door, the sill or lower part of the wooden frame of a door; a vestibule, a porch; a raised terrace in front of a door (see ḍeʼoṛhī).

ety: Prk. देअघरं or देअघरअं; S. देव+गृहं or गृह+कं
def: s.m. An idol temple (Hindū or Jain).
adj. & s.m. Having a body, embodied, corporeal; of or belonging to the body; — a living creature or being, a man ( = deh-vān).
s.f. = deh, q.v.
adj. = dehī, q.v.
s.m. Land flooded after the rains, marshy land.

ety: Prk. देअघरं or देअघरअं; S. देव+गृहं or गृह+कं
def: s.m. An idol temple (Hindū or Jain).
s.f. Marshy soil.
s.f. = dehlī, q.v.
s.f. A volley (of musketry).
s.f. = ḍeʼoṛhī, q.v.
s.f. = ḍeʼoṛhī, q.v.
s.m. Dowry, portion; presents made by the bride's family to the bridegroom with the view of securing a husband of higher rank.
s.m. Part of a portion or dowry.
s.m. (f. -ī), Seeing, sight
subs: s.f. Looking, scanning; ogling; searching, search

s.f. 'Deception or fascination of the sight (as effected by conjurers, &c.), ocular deception or mistake; a winding place or passage (as the course of a river, &c.); any intricate or perplexing work; a maze, labyrinth (cf. dekhat-bhulī)

s.f. Seeing or looking at one another, mutual gazing or scanning; the being within sight (of an object); — imitation, emulation, competition, rivalry; — adv. In sight (of); by seeing, at the sight (of, -kī, or terī, &c.), in imitation (of), through rivalry
v.n. To keep looking (at), to continue looking, be in the habit of looking.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Seen, &c.
subs: part. adj. (f. -ī), Seen and experienced or tried
adj. (f. -ī), Seen, perceived, observed, experienced, suffered, endured, &c.

def: s.f. = dikhāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Seeing, looking at, &c.
v.t. = dikhānā, q.v.

ety: imperf. part. of dekhnā, and = Prk. दक्खंतं and दक्खंतअं; see dekhnā
def: part. adj. & adv. Seeing; — whilst seeing or looking at; at sight (of)
subs: s.f. Deception or fascination of the sight, ocular deception; — a maze, labyrinth; — a kind of convoluted and intricate embroidery work on net or muslin
adv. To look at, to all appearance
adv. Before the eyes, in the presence (of, -ke, or -mere, &c.); — through long or continued looking
v.n. To stand still looking, to stand gazing (in wonder, or disappointment, &c.), to remain agape
v.n. To keep on the watch, to look (after); to continue looking.

def: v.t. = dikhlānā, q.v.

ety: dekh˚ = देक्ख(इ) = S. द्रक्ष्य(ति), the future of rt. दृश् (used in the sense of the present)
def: v.t. To see, look, look at, behold, view, observe, perceive, inspect, mark, note, consider, look to, weigh well, examine, prove, try; to search, scan; to watch (for); to feel (as the pulse, &c.); to experience, suffer, endure, tolerate, bear the sight of
subs: To see, look at, view, inspect, examine, try
v.n. To come into sight, to be seen, to appear, be visible; to be observed; to be experienced, &c.
v.t. To be permitted to see, to get to see; to find out, to ascertain
v.n. To be seen, to be or become visible, to appear; to seem

def: intj. See! behold! observe! lo! look here! look out! be on your guard! (2nd per. pl. aorist) you'll see; perhaps.

ety: imperf. part. of dekhnā, and = Prk. दक्खंतं and दक्खंतअं; see dekhnā
def: part. adj. & adv. Seeing; — whilst seeing or looking at; at sight (of)
subs: s.f. Deception or fascination of the sight, ocular deception; — a maze, labyrinth; — a kind of convoluted and intricate embroidery work on net or muslin
adv. To look at, to all appearance
adv. Before the eyes, in the presence (of, -ke, or -mere, &c.); — through long or continued looking
v.n. To stand still looking, to stand gazing (in wonder, or disappointment, &c.), to remain agape
v.n. To keep on the watch, to look (after); to continue looking.

def: s.m. Seer, beholder, viewer, observer, on-looker, spectator ( = dekhne-hārā).
s.m. Lump (of earth, &c.), clod; bump; corn; bunion; — ploughed land ( = ḍhepā).
s.m. = ḍhelā, q.v.
s.m. = dihlī or děhlī, q.v.
v.t. See s.v. denā.
s.m. (f. ?), Giving; a gift debt, liability ( = denā)
subs: (or len-den), s.m. 'Giving and taking,' dealings, commercial transactions, barter, traffic; pecuniary transactions on interest; debit and credit
den-hārā, s.m. Giver ( = dene-wālā); payer; debtor.

ety: de˚ = Prk. दे(इ) = S. ददा(ति), rt. दा, or S. दीय(ते), passive of दा
def: v.t. To give, grant, impart, yield, produce, afford, emit; to lay (eggs); to resign; to pay (a debt, &c.); to cause, occasion (as pain, &c.); to allow, let, permit (after an inflected infinitive, e.g. jāne denā); to place, put (on, or in or into, -meṅ; e.g. gale-meṅ hāth denā; pānī-meṅ zahr denā); to put (on), apply; to give forth, to raise (a cry for help, &c.); to close (a door); — s.m. Giving; paying; that which is to pay, debt, liability, &c.
subs: s.m. Assets and liabilities, debit and credit, profit and loss; settling (one's) affairs
v.t. To give away (de-ḍālnā); to make over, deliver, consign; to give up, resign, concede
v.t. To give away, to bestow
v.t. To dash down, to dash (against, -par), to throw; to stamp

(with acc.), To leave at death, to bequeath. (N.B. Not only is the root or the past conj. part. of denā prefixed to another verb to intensify or to modify its meaning, as in de-ḍālnā, de-marnā, &c.; but the verb itself is commonly added to the base of another verb to intensify its sense, e.g. thakā-denā, 'to tire out'; pheṅk-denā, 'to throw away': it is however frequently added without affecting the sense of the other verb; and such a compound is even more common than the simple verb.)
